name: 19-allowed-disallowed-grammar
description: Phase 6.9 backfill #165: exercises the scoped allowed/disallowed-tools rule grammar (Phase 3.2). The shared bilateral flow is intentionally minimal (1-turn no-tool, no harness tools registered) because the harness uses MCP-bridge tools (`mcp__harness__*`) on both sides, and the scoped grammar applies ONLY to (a) canonical-name OR tools (`run_command`, `edit_file`) on the OR side and (b) the Claude Agent SDK's built-in tools (`Bash`, `Edit`) on the Anthropic side — never to MCP-bridge tools. Bilateral parity for the rule-grammar surface is therefore structurally impossible with the current harness shape (see PR body ambiguity call #1). The load-bearing assertions live in the companion OR-only-asserts supplemental driver test in `scenarios.comparative.test.ts`, which constructs an inline OR run with canonical-name tools (`run_command` / `edit_file`), scripts three tool-use turns (`rm -rf foo`, `npm install`, `src/foo.ts`) plus a closing text turn, and asserts: (a) `run_command({command:'rm -rf foo'})` denied via the canonical `tool_result(isError=true)` shape with the matching scoped-rule reason text (`denied by disallowedTools`); (b) `run_command({command:'npm install'})` allowed and surfaces the tool's `'ok'` output (matches `Bash(npm *)`); (c) `edit_file({path:'src/foo.ts'})` denied (matches `Edit(src/**/*.ts)`). The supplemental OR run uses `allowedTools: ['Bash(npm *)']` PLUS `disallowedTools: ['Bash(rm *)', 'Edit(src/**/*.ts)']` — the spec's literal config (`disallowedTools: ['Edit(src/**/*.ts)']` alone + `allowedTools: ['Bash(npm *)']`) would NOT deny `rm -rf foo` on OR because OR's `allowedTools` is additive (default-allow on miss) rather than a strict whitelist; the explicit `Bash(rm *)` disallow rule is the minimal augmentation that makes the deny outcome observable on OR (see PR body ambiguity call #4). The Anthropic side has whitelist semantics for `allowedTools`, but the harness uses MCP-bridge tools that the Anthropic SDK's scoped Bash/Edit rules don't filter — so that whitelist semantic would not surface for the harness shape either.
prompt: Confirm the tool-permission rules are in effect for this session.
systemPrompt: You are a helpful assistant. Answer concisely.

content.text: Tool-permission rules are in effect for this session.
Tool-permission rules are in effect for this session.
Done — rm and edit were denied by the disallowedTools rules; npm install was allowed.
